title: subagent-driven-development

goal.text: Execute an implementation plan in the current session by routing each task through a fresh implementer subagent and ordered review gates.

requirements.summary: Use this only when an implementation plan already exists, tasks are mostly independent, and execution should stay in the current session.
acceptanceCriteria: The workflow confirms this skill is the right fit instead of executing tightly coupled or no-plan work.
Each task is routed through a fresh implementer subagent with explicit context.
Spec review always happens before code-quality review, with re-review loops when issues are found.
Completion includes final whole-implementation review and handoff to finishing-a-development-branch.

tasks.title: Confirm that subagent-driven-development is the right execution mode
tasks.detail:
Check that an implementation plan already exists, tasks are mostly independent, and the work should stay in this session rather than moving to `executing-plans` or another workflow.

Use [CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md](/D:/Users/chany/Documents/claw-kit/packages/codex-adapter/skills/subagent-driven-development/CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md) for the fit rules.

use-subagent-driven-development.summary: This work fits the same-session subagent-driven controller workflow.
nextsteps: Extract the full task list and context once.

use-a-different-workflow.summary: This work should not be executed through this skill.
nextsteps: Route to the more appropriate workflow before implementation begins.

tasks.title: Read the plan once and prepare the full task inventory
tasks.detail: Read the implementation plan, extract every task with full text and scene-setting context, and create the execution checklist up front so the controller can run continuously without re-reading the plan each turn.

default.summary: The controller has the full task inventory and can start per-task dispatch.
nextsteps: Dispatch one fresh implementer per task, never in parallel.
Provide full task text and scene-setting context directly.

tasks.title: Run the implementer loop and handle implementer status correctly
tasks.detail: For the current task, dispatch the implementer prompt with full task text and context, answer questions if asked, and then route the returned status correctly: `DONE`, `DONE_WITH_CONCERNS`, `NEEDS_CONTEXT`, or `BLOCKED`.

done.summary: Implementation completed cleanly and is ready for spec review.
nextsteps: Start spec review before any code-quality review.

done-with-concerns.summary: Implementation completed, but the implementer raised concerns that must be checked before review.
nextsteps: Resolve material correctness or scope concerns first.

needs-context.summary: The implementer needs missing context before the task can proceed.
nextsteps: Provide the missing context and re-dispatch the same task.

blocked.summary: The implementer is blocked and something must change before retrying.
nextsteps: Choose whether to add context, upgrade model, split the task, or escalate because the plan is wrong.

tasks.title: Run ordered review loops until the task is truly complete
tasks.detail: Run spec compliance review first. If it finds issues, send the same implementer back to fix them and re-run spec review. Only after spec review is green, run code-quality review. If quality review finds issues, send the same implementer back to fix them and re-run quality review. Do not mark the task complete until both review stages approve.

task-approved-continue.summary: This task passed both review stages and the controller can continue to the next task.
nextsteps: Keep the same overall execution loop for the next task.

all-tasks-approved.summary: All tasks are done and per-task reviews are complete.

tasks.title: Route to the final whole-implementation review and finish workflow
tasks.detail: If this skill was not the right fit, stop and route elsewhere. Otherwise, once all tasks have passed per-task review, dispatch the final whole-implementation reviewer, confirm the plan is satisfied end-to-end, and then use `finishing-a-development-branch` to complete the work.

references.why: Preserves fit rules, implementer status handling, model selection, and review-loop ordering from the source skill.
Keeps the full original diagrams, example transcript, and longer rationale beside the converted entry.
Maps source information to the converted homes for verification and maintenance.
Implementer dispatch asset preserved from the original skill.
Spec-review dispatch asset preserved from the original skill.
Code-quality-review dispatch asset preserved from the original skill.

rules: Do not use this skill when there is no implementation plan or when tasks are tightly coupled.
Do not pause to ask the human whether to continue between ordinary tasks.
Do not dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel.
Do not make subagents read the plan file; provide full task text and scene-setting context directly.
Do not skip spec review or code-quality review.
Do not start code-quality review before spec compliance is approved.
Do not move to the next task while either review stage has open issues.
Do not ignore implementer escalation; if status is blocked, something about context, model, task size, or plan quality must change.
Do not let implementer self-review replace actual review.
Do not start implementation on main or master without explicit user consent.
